# UC-22.14.12 · Monitoring for Information Disclosure via DLP and Web Exfil Patterns

## Description

Correlates DLP policy violations and large outbound transfers to evidence AU-13 monitoring for information disclosure to unauthorized parties.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes o365 and sourcetypes ms:o365:management. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Ensure unified audit ingestion includes DLP operations; (2) enrich with `UserId` to HR directory via lookup; (3) threshold alerts for rare operations; (4) cross-check with firewall `bytes_out` spikes for same user within 1h (separate correlation). Indexes required: `index=o365`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:management`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `ms:o365:management` DLP workloads, `pan:traffic` bytes_out.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ensure unified audit ingestion includes DLP operations; (2) enrich with `UserId` to HR directory via lookup; (3) threshold alerts for rare operations; (4) cross-check with firewall `bytes_out` spikes for same user within 1h (separate correlation).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management Workload="DLP" earliest=-24h
| stats count by Operation, UserId, ObjectId
| sort - count
| head 100
```

Understanding this SPL

**Monitoring for Information Disclosure via DLP and Web Exfil Patterns (AU-13)** — Correlates DLP policy violations and large outbound transfers to evidence AU-13 monitoring for information disclosure to unauthorized parties.

Documented **Data sources**: `ms:o365:management` DLP workloads, `pan:traffic` bytes_out. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: o365; **sourcetype**: ms:o365:management. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=o365, sourcetype=ms:o365:management, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by Operation, UserId, ObjectId** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (top DLP operations), Time chart (violations), Link to firewall dashboard

## SPL

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management Workload="DLP" earliest=-24h
| stats count by Operation, UserId, ObjectId
| sort - count
| head 100
```

## Visualization

Table (top DLP operations), Time chart (violations), Link to firewall dashboard. Key fields: `ObjectId`, `Operation`, `UserId`.
